dismissalText.commentary: Tim Seifert c Phillips b Rabada 19 (14b 2x4 1x6 21m) SR: 135.71

dismissalComment.html: Bashed straight to Phillips at cover! What have you done, Seifert? Was a nothing ball, really, outside off and lots of width off a good length. But the ball died just a little with its bounce off the pitch. Seifert reaches for the cut and times it to perfection. Perfectly to Phillips, that is...

dismissalText.commentary: Ajinkya Rahane c Rabada b Mohammed Siraj 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: Rahane's gone for it, and consequently, he's gone.. The ball was full and right outside off. Rahane goes for a lofted drive right down the ground. Except he doesn't middle this - the swing got the better of him - so there's a top edge that looms in the air forever, then lands right in Rabada's hands near mid-on

dismissalText.long: c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma
dismissalText.commentary: Rovman Powell c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma 27 (20b 2x4 2x6 36m) SR: 135

dismissalComment.html: from 146kph to 107kph - and a wicket arrives. From the back of the hand from Ashok, who bowls on ten shorter side of a length just outside off. Powell has a big heave by reaching out - he may have been beaten by the lack of pace - and ends up skying the ball really high. Siraj, from long-on, does well to concentrate and keep his eye on it as he moves forward. He ends up taking it a good few steps forward

dismissalText.commentary: Anukul Roy c †Buttler b Prasidh Krishna 9 (7b 1x4 0x6 22m) SR: 128.57

dismissalComment.html: Wow, Prasidh and co, and the umpire, heard an outside edge, leaving me confused!! There's a reason why I'm not an umpire!! Short of a length from Prasidh, and on off at 142kph. The ball seams away, and Anukul looks to loft hard. But the ball takes a thin outside edge to Buttler, and Prasidh is pumped up before the umpire raises his finger

dismissalComment.html: a faint outside edge again, and Rinku is done. Buttler and Rabada went up as soon as the ball left the bat. It was on a short of a length just outside off, even as the ball shaped away a little. Rinku went lofting but his front foot was around middle or leg. So he ended up playing away from his body, and got a thin edge

dismissalText.long: c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma
dismissalText.commentary: Ramandeep Singh c Mohammed Siraj b Ashok Sharma 17 (8b 2x4 1x6 6m) SR: 212.5

dismissalComment.html: taken at long-on!! Ashok gets one off the slower ball again. And the fielder, yet again, is Siraj. Ashok floats this at just 105kph, and goes on a length around sixth or seventh stump. Raman can't wait for the ball to arrive, and has a huge heave at it to go across the line. He ends up ballooning the ball for Siraj, who's at long-on, and gets across to his right to grab it

dismissalComment.html: well taken by Phillips - the best man on the park!! He was at wide backward point, if ever there was such a position. And he moved well to his left, and ended up close to deep-backward point, all the while keeping his eyes on the ball. He slid and completed the catch, as Narine went for a slice off this fullish ball around off

dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill c Green b Arora 86 (50b 8x4 4x6 83m) SR: 172

dismissalComment.html: amazing catch by Green!! Gill falls on 86 after a tumbling take by the tallest man on the field. Green is at deep third, and he moves and dives forward to take it barely off the ground. Gill walks off livid with himself. Arora kept firing it full and a little wide of off, and this time Gill couldn't find the gap. He went slicing to go through the line, and sliced it aerially to deep third. A shorter fielder there, and you think Gill may have survived
